perturbation.original_text: You and your Representatives (i) will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you or your affiliates and (ii) will keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with the paragraph titled “Compelled Disclosure” below) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any information in the Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, will be provided with a copy of this Agreement and will agree to be bound by the terms hereof).
perturbation.changed_text: You and your Agents (i) will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you or your affiliates and (ii) will keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with the paragraph titled “Compelled Disclosure” below) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any information in the Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, will be provided with a copy of this Agreement and will agree to be bound by the terms hereof).
perturbation.explanation: In the phrase "You and your Representatives", the term "Representatives" is replaced with "Agents". However, later in the same section, the text refers back to the original definition of "Representatives", with (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction". The contradiction lies in failing to consistently use the new term 'Agents'. It is now unclear whether the term Agents is the same as Representatives or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Agents (i) will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you or your affiliates and (ii) will keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with the paragraph titled “Compelled Disclosure” below) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any information in the Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, will be provided with a copy of this Agreement and will agree to be bound by the terms hereof).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Representatives' with 'Agents' in the initial part of the sentence. However, the subsequent clause refers back to 'Representatives', creating a contradiction. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, because the contradiction is in the same place where the change occurs. It's now unclear whether 'Agents' and 'Representatives' are interchangeable, creating a strong internal inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: You will make all reasonable, necessary and appropriate efforts to safeguard Evaluation FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 2 Material from disclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby. You agree to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your Representatives (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto).
perturbation.changed_text: You will make reasonable efforts to safeguard Evaluation FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 2 Material from disclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby. You agree to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your Affiliates (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto).
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "all reasonable, necessary and appropriate efforts" is shortened to "reasonable efforts," reducing the standard of care expected. The term "Representatives" is changed to "Affiliates", with the definition of Affiliates never defined in this agreement. The sentence then refers back to 'Representatives' creating an in-text contradiction in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will make reasonable efforts to safeguard Evaluation FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 2 Material from disclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby. You agree to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your Affiliates (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'all reasonable, necessary and appropriate efforts' to 'reasonable efforts' lowers the standard of care, but the most significant contradiction arises from changing 'Representatives' to 'Affiliates' while the text still refers to 'Representatives'. Because 'Affiliates' is never defined within the agreement, the second half of the sentence becomes unclear as to whose actions the party is responsible for. This creates an in-text contradiction because it creates an ambiguity as to the definition of 'Affiliates' and 'Representatives', which would not exist if the text remained 'Representatives'. The Contradicted Text and Changed Text are in the same location which helps exemplify the point of confusion, as according to the prompt, the Changed Text and Contradicted Text can be located within the same location. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that you are requested or required to disclose all or any part of the information contained in the Evaluation Material pursuant to the terms of a valid and effective subpoena or order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or a federal, state or local governmental or regulatory body or FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 3 pursuant to a civil investigative demand or similar judicial process or otherwise, to the extent permitted by applicable law or cognizant authority, you will (i) notify the Company in writing immediately of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such request or requirement, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request or requirement, (iii) if disclosure of any such information is required, disclose only that portion of the information which, upon written advice of your legal counsel, you are legally required to disclose and (iv) exercise your best efforts to obtain a protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such information. In any event, you will cooperate with the Company to obtain such a protective order or other assurance. Any expense you incur in connection with the Company’s desire to narrow or limit the scope of the request or requirement shall be the responsibility of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that you are requested or required to disclose all or any part of the data contained in the Evaluation Material pursuant to the terms of a valid and effective subpoena or order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or a federal, state or local governmental or regulatory body or FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 3 pursuant to a civil investigative demand or similar judicial process or otherwise, to the extent permitted by applicable law or cognizant authority, you will (i) notify the Company in writing immediately of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such request or requirement, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request or requirement, (iii) if disclosure of any such information is required, disclose only that portion of the information which, upon written advice of your legal counsel, you are legally required to disclose and (iv) exercise your best efforts to obtain a protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such information. In any event, you will cooperate with the Company to obtain such a protective order or other assurance. Any expense you incur in connection with the Company’s desire to narrow or limit the scope of the request or requirement shall be the responsibility of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: In the event that you are requested or required to disclose all or any part of the information contained in the Evaluation Material the phrase "information" is replaced with "data." This creates ambiguity as to whether all data is information, or all information is data. This creates confusion on whether this section extends to more than just data, if information is seen as a bigger set.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that you are requested or required to disclose all or any part of the data contained in the Evaluation Material pursuant to the terms of a valid and effective subpoena or order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or a federal, state or local governmental or regulatory body or FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 3 pursuant to a civil investigative demand or similar judicial process or otherwise, to the extent permitted by applicable law or cognizant authority, you will (i) notify the Company in writing immediately of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such request or requirement, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request or requirement, (iii) if disclosure of any such information is required, disclose only that portion of the information which, upon written advice of your legal counsel, you are legally required to disclose and (iv) exercise your best efforts to obtain a protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such information. In any event, you will cooperate with the Company to obtain such a protective order or other assurance. Any expense you incur in connection with the Company’s desire to narrow or limit the scope of the request or requirement shall be the responsibility of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "information" with "data" in a section concerning disclosure obligations. The explanation asserts that this substitution creates ambiguity because it's unclear if 'data' and 'information' are equivalent. This ambiguity leads to a contradiction within the document. The contradicted text is a repetition of the changed text, which reaffirms that the inconsistency exists within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not, directly or indirectly, and you will cause any person or entity controlled by you not to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Company, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its affiliates, or any securities or contract rights (other than broadly based index funds) the terms or value of which are dependent on securities of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into, directly or indirectly, any merger, consolidation, recapitalization, business combination, partnership, joint venture or other similar transaction involving the Company or any of its affiliates, (iii) make, or in any way participate in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any of its affiliates, (iv) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the 1934 Act) with respect to any voting securities of the Company or any of its affiliates, (v) negotiate, have any discussions or enter into any arrangements, understandings or agreements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, finance, assist or encourage, any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing, or, make any investment in FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 4 any other person that engages, or offers or proposes to engage, in any of the foregoing (it being understood that, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you shall not be permitted to act as a joint bidder or co-bidder with any other person with respect to the Company), (vi) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vii) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (viii) advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not, directly or indirectly, and you will cause any person or entity controlled by you not to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Organization, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its affiliates, or any securities or contract rights (other than broadly based index funds) the terms or value of which are dependent on securities of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into, directly or indirectly, any merger, consolidation, recapitalization, business combination, partnership, joint venture or other similar transaction involving the Company or any of its affiliates, (iii) make, or in any way participate in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any of its affiliates, (iv) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the 1934 Act) with respect to any voting securities of the Company or any of its affiliates, (v) negotiate, have any discussions or enter into any arrangements, understandings or agreements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, finance, assist or encourage, any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing, or, make any investment in FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 4 any other person that engages, or offers or proposes to engage, in any of the foregoing (it being understood that, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you shall not be permitted to act as a joint bidder or co-bidder with any other person with respect to the Company), (vi) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vii) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (viii) advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the "Board of Directors of the Company" to "Board of Directors of the Organization" and not defining Organization. It is now uncertain whether they are talking about the Board of Directors of the Company or a different Organization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not, directly or indirectly, and you will cause any person or entity controlled by you not to, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of the Organization, (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, any securities or property of the Company or any of its affiliates, or any securities or contract rights (other than broadly based index funds) the terms or value of which are dependent on securities of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into, directly or indirectly, any merger, consolidation, recapitalization, business combination, partnership, joint venture or other similar transaction involving the Company or any of its affiliates, (iii) make, or in any way participate in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any of its affiliates, (iv) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the 1934 Act) with respect to any voting securities of the Company or any of its affiliates, (v) negotiate, have any discussions or enter into any arrangements, understandings or agreements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, finance, assist or encourage, any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing, or, make any investment in FLIR Systems, Inc. March 17, 2010 Page 4 any other person that engages, or offers or proposes to engage, in any of the foregoing (it being understood that, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you shall not be permitted to act as a joint bidder or co-bidder with any other person with respect to the Company), (vi) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vii) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (viii) advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'Board of Directors of the Company'. The changed text alters this to 'Board of Directors of the Organization'. Because the term 'Organization' is undefined and used interchangeably with 'Company' within the same clause, this creates ambiguity as to which entity's Board of Directors is being referenced. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as it introduces a new term without defining it, leading to uncertainty and potential conflicting interpretations of the obligations. The contradicted text, which uses the modified text, inherits this ambiguity, reinforcing the in-text contradiction. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
